prompt: Operator: DropoutDoMask apply phase: out = data * mask * scale where scale = 1/keep_prob. Inputs: data (float16) and a dense float16 keep-mask of {0.,1.} (same shape as data); keep_prob is a host scalar folded into scale (asc.ConstExpr[float]). Input shapes: [16, 2048], [32, 4096]; perf shape [32, 4096]. Output shape: same_as_input. Dtype: float16. Layout: contiguous. Axis: null. Tiling: flatten to 1D in the host launcher; the kernel takes `size: int`, two GlobalAddress inputs (data, mask) + one output, tiles 1D with `asc2.tensor(ptr, [size])`, two `asc2.load(..., [tile_size], offsets=[off])`, `out = (data_t * mask_t) * scale`, `asc2.store(...)`. PERF: WIDE tile TILE_SIZE=2048, CORE_NUM=16, double-buffer via `asc2.range(tile_per_block, unroll_factor=2, parallel=True)`; size must be a multiple of TILE_SIZE*CORE_NUM=32768 (aligned_only). This two-load masked multiply lands ratio ~0.74 vs hand-written aclnnDropoutDoMask at [32, 4096]. Comparability: the canonical reference bit-unpacks a uint8 mask; the dense float16 keep-mask here has identical per-element multiply+scale cost (the dominant term). Tail: aligned_only. Accumulator: null. Tolerance: atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2. Platform: Ascend950PR_9599. Build/run: python3.11 kernel.py -r Model -v Ascend950PR_9599. Workaround: Reference golden/kernels/drop_out_do_mask_f16.py for the proven two-input wide-tile structure and the dense-mask comparability note.
